part_b_gateway_path_phase1.scope: Phase 1 pure verdict core ONLY — clock-injected, offline, fixture-tested. No SSH, no network, no scheduler, no bin/CLI wiring (lib.rs only, as the TLS core at slice 2a). Mirrors the lease-presence core structure.
part_b_gateway_path_phase1.non_lift: dpinger gateway report = pfSenseRuntimeReport (dpinger is itself an active probe); NQ path probe from a named vantage = ObservedReachability; a mismatch is path ambiguity, NEVER WAN-down / ISP-outage / internet-down / user-impact.
verdict_ladder: gateway_not_reported_up (down/unknown: no up-claim to corroborate; records the box report, does not witness an outage)
cannot_testify_no_path_basis (up but no path probe attempted)
gateway_uncorroborated_path_fails (THE specimen: up + path attempted + not reached = path ambiguity)
gateway_corroborated_by_path (up + path reached this target from this vantage at this time; nothing stronger)
part_b_gateway_path_phase1.design_notes: Degraded (dpinger packetloss/high-latency warning) still claims a path is up -> unreached path is the uncorroborated specimen, not a pass. RTT/loss carried on GatewayReport for context only, never a service-quality verdict; GatewayReport/receipt drop Eq because they carry f64. Receipt-only; no is_ok()/healthy/green coercion; scope_ceiling_non_claims carries the refusals.
part_b_gateway_path_phase1.tests: 10 offline fixtures (corroborated via ICMP reach; the uncorroborated-path-fails specimen; cannot-testify on no-basis and declared-not-run; not-up has no up-claim even with a failing path; degraded still claims up; mixed-reach wins; injected-clock probe_time; passive-vs-active perturbation; refusals-and-no-coercion; testimony-type travels).
part_b_gateway_path_phase1.verification: set -o pipefail; cargo test -p nq-monitor -> EXIT=0, 167 lib tests, 0 failed, 0 compiler warnings. (exit code is the verdict, not eyeballed.)
part_b_gateway_path_phase1.gated_next: Phase 2 live read (NOT started, operator go required): read dpinger gateway status over the existing read-only SSH path + run an external-vantage ICMP/TCP/HTTP path probe wired to the core. Interesting vantage is EXTERNAL (off-LAN) so 'path fails' isn't a LAN-egress quirk; perturbation ledger applies (external probe may be flagged as a scan upstream).
